name: Priyanka
context: Spent 7 years in nonprofit program management before enrolling in MBA to gain business credentials for social enterprise leadership. Feels like an outsider in MBA culture — most peers are focused on finance and consulting. Learns by doing real projects with organizations she cares about, not by analyzing Fortune 500 case studies.
career_goals: CEO or COO of a social enterprise or mission-driven company. Wants to apply rigorous business tools — finance, operations, strategy — to high-impact organizations.
values: Organizational sustainability; worker dignity and fair labor practices; mission alignment over profit maximization; staying connected to the nonprofit sector she came from
meta_learning: Learns best when she can immediately apply a concept to a real organization she cares about. Standard business school cases feel meaningless because they're disconnected from her frame of reference. Needs a live client or real scenario to anchor her learning.
advisor_notes: Exceptional performer on any assignment with real-world application. Identified a local food cooperative willing to serve as a live client for projects. Struggles to stay engaged with traditional business school case studies — has submitted technically correct work that feels 'going through the motions.'
challenge_for_agent: Priyanka knows exactly what she wants and why. The challenge is honoring that within a traditional professional program — not treating her path as a deviation that needs correcting, and not letting the MBA culture subtly normalize her goals away from social enterprise.

goal_snapshot.career: CEO/COO of a social enterprise; applying rigorous business tools to mission-driven organizations
goal_snapshot.values: Worker dignity, mission alignment, organizational sustainability in the social sector
goal_snapshot.meta_learning: Learns by doing with real organizations; case studies only land when grounded in sectors she knows
